howto_rules: Handle missing repo root gracefully with RepoRootLocatedCheck
Set realistic timeouts on build/run commands
Support verify/command build modes via env var
example_patterns: find_repo_root scanning workspace and immediate children for marker files
FilesystemPathCheck for build outputs and datasets
ListSimilarityCheck with Pearson correlation for experiment results

borrowed_structure.artifact_build.py: verify/command mode toggle pattern from egwalker
borrowed_structure.benchmark_prep.py: FilesystemPathCheck for benchmark directories from egwalker
borrowed_structure.experiment_runs.py: FilesystemPathCheck for output directories, no numeric comparison since Herbie outputs HTML not JSON

artifact_specific_adaptations: Check racket version instead of rustc/cargo/node
Check rust version as optional (only needed for source build)
Build via 'make install' or 'raco pkg install --auto herbie'
Verify herbie binary or src/herbie.rkt accessible after build
Benchmark directories bench/ and bench/hamming/ are in-tree, not downloaded
Experiment outputs are HTML report directories (report/, graphs/), not JSON
Unit tests via 'raco test src/' produce pass/fail, not numeric data
No numeric reference comparison needed - check output directory existence and non-emptiness
plan_summary: Herbie is a Racket-based tool for improving floating-point expression accuracy. Environment requires Racket 8.0+ (mandatory) and Rust 1.60.0+ (optional, for source build). Build via 'make install'. Benchmarks are in-tree under bench/. Experiments produce HTML report directories. No numeric reference data comparison is needed - we check that the experiment output directories exist and contain files.

step_coverage.env-003: Covered implicitly by platform running the oracle
step_coverage.build-001: BuildCommandCheck for 'make install' in command mode; verify mode checks herbie binary or src/herbie.rkt
step_coverage.build-002: Not separately checked; covered by build-001 verify mode since both methods produce same result
step_coverage.run-001: FilesystemPathCheck for report/ or graphs/ as evidence that herbie ran
step_coverage.run-002: FilesystemPathCheck for report/ directory with non-empty check
step_coverage.run-003: FilesystemPathCheck for graphs/ directory with non-empty check

step_gaps: env-003: Hardware/platform check (x86-64 vs ARM) cannot be operationalized as a portable check
build-002: Alternative install method not separately tested; agent may use either method
run-001: Unit test pass/fail not captured as structured data; we check for experiment output directories instead
step_augmentations: Added check for Makefile presence in repo root as repo marker
Added non-empty directory check for report/graphs output dirs
Added optional check for make tool availability
